summary: Mellow mountain vibe with standout Watchtower‑Stella Blue stretch
blurb: The show delivers a relaxed, high‑energy second set highlighted by an all‑along‑the‑Watchtower/​Stella Blue stretch and tight China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition. Jerry’s guitar riffs are on fire while Brent shines on Cassidy and Push Comes To Shove.
review: On August 20, 1987 at Park West Ski Resort the Dead served up a breezy, mountain‑side set that rewards any listener with its relaxed flow and bursts of brilliance. The first set rolls through familiar terrain—Big Boss Man, The Promised Land, Dire Wolf and New Minglewood Blues—but it’s the second half where the magic truly kicks in. A superb China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue launches a sequence that builds into a smoking All Along The Watchtower leading straight into an emotive Stella Blue, a moment repeatedly called “sublime” and “on fire” by reviewers. Brent Mydland’s keyboards twinkle on Cassidy and hit peak Keith‑like intensity on When Push Comes To Shove, while Phil Lesh provides solid, grounding bass lines throughout. The drums from Mickey and Bill are described as intense and perfectly locked into the outdoor vibe. Though a few songs (Big Boss Man, Truckin’, Promised Land) received lukewarm reactions and one matrix mix mutes Bob’s guitar in places, the overall performance is consistently praised for its relaxed yet high‑energy feel. The recommended listening experience comes from an excellent audience tape that captures the ambience with amazing clarity, making this show a must‑hear snapshot of the 1987 summer tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (3 positive, 3 mixed) highlight strong performances—especially Watchtower/​Stella Blue, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and Brent’s keyboards—while only occasional criticisms of a few songs or mix issues appear. The balance leans solidly positive, meriting a +1.0 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience recording praised for amazing, crystal‑clear sound that captures the full show with superior preservation compared to older tapes.

key_highlights: All Along The Watchtower → Stella Blue stretch stands out as a smoking highlight.
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition delivers tight, melodic energy.
Brent’s keyboard work shines on Cassidy and When Push Comes To Shove.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia rips guitar on Watchtower, Loser and Stella Blue; his tone on New Minglewood Blues is praised even when the matrix mix sometimes drowns it out, delivering a consistently fiery lead throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides solid, present bass—from tuning into the Zeppelin‑style intro on China Cat to powerful work on The Wheel—earning steady praise with no notable criticisms.
band_performance.Bob: Weir lays down dependable rhythm guitar; his slide work adds nastiness when audible, though the matrix mix occasionally mutes his parts. Otherwise he’s solid throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland shines with twinkling keys on Cassidy and a Keith‑like burst on When Push Comes To Shove, supporting the second set jams and adding texture to the mellow vibe.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart delivers an intense drum performance that drives the outdoor energy, highlighted as “intense” during the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming meshes with Hart for a powerful, high‑energy rhythm section, contributing to the show’s overall intensity.
